summary: This notebook serves as a practical tutorial on integrating the OpenAI API within Kaggle notebooks. It walks readers through generating an API key, securely storing it using Kaggle's built-in secrets manager, and writing a cross-platform helper function to retrieve it. The code demonstrates initializing the OpenAI client and sending a text prompt to the gpt-4o-mini model to generate a completion. Readers should learn how to safely handle credentials in Kaggle environments and make basic API calls without exposing sensitive data.
competition_flow: Demonstrates how to securely store and retrieve an OpenAI API key on Kaggle, initialize the OpenAI client, and send a text prompt to generate a model completion.
insights: Kaggle provides a native secrets manager that can be accessed programmatically via the kaggle_secrets package.
A single helper function can be written to abstract secret retrieval across both Kaggle and Google Colab environments.
The OpenAI Python SDK supports direct integration with Kaggle secrets for secure, production-like API usage.
